IsA: It is a series of murals.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by depicting scenes from Alabama’s agricultural history.
It is characterized by illustrating key periods of Alabama’s agricultural development.
It is characterized by beginning with Native American farming practices.
It is characterized by progressing through later agricultural developments.
It is characterized by being part of a broader WPA initiative.
It is characterized by being created with tempera, a water-based medium.
It is characterized by its murals being approximately 7.5 feet by 5.5 feet.
UsedFor: It is used for depicting scenes from Alabama’s agricultural history.
It is used for illustrating key periods of Alabama’s agricultural development.
It is used for celebrating the past.
It is used for focusing farmers on a vision of the future.
It is used for educational outreach.
It is used for showcasing Alabama agriculture.
It is used for public display.
It is used for a sesquicentennial celebration.
LocatedIn: It is located at the Alabama State Fair.
It is located in Birmingham, Alabama.
It is located in Shreveport, Louisiana.
It is located at the Louisiana State Fair.
It is located in Duncan Hall at Auburn University.
It is located in Foy Student Union on the Auburn University campus.
It is located in the Foy Union Student Union Gallery.
OriginatedIn: It originated in the Alabama Extension Service.
It originated in the Alabama State Fair.
CreatedBy: It was created by John Augustus Walker.
CreatedIn: It was created in Mobile, Alabama.
It was created in Auburn, Alabama.
DiscoveredIn: It was discovered in the early 1980s.
ProducedBy: It was produced by the Alabama Extension Service.
It was produced by the Works Progress Administration (WPA).
CausedBy: It is caused by the need to showcase Alabama agriculture.
It is caused by a desire for educational outreach.
It is caused by a desire to support artists during the Great Depression.
ConsistsOf: It consists of scenes from Alabama’s agricultural history.
It consists of illustrations of key periods of Alabama’s agricultural development.
PartOf: It is part of a broader WPA initiative.
It is part of the Alabama State Fair.
It is part of Auburn University's sesquicentennial celebration.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the Alabama Cooperative Extension System.
KnownFor: It is known for being a series of murals.
It is known for depicting Alabama's agricultural history.
It is known for being a WPA-sponsored public art project.
It is known for its historical accuracy concerns.
It is known for its rediscovery in the early 1980s.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Alabama Extension Service.
It is associated with the Works Progress Administration (WPA).
It is associated with the Alabama State Fair.
It is associated with John Augustus Walker.
It is associated with P. O. Davis.
It is associated with Vice President Warren Leech.
It is associated with H.T. Baldwin.
It is associated with Donald Robertson.
It is associated with the Great Depression era.
It is associated with Auburn University.
HeldIn: It was held in Birmingham, Alabama.
It was held in Shreveport, Louisiana.
About: It is about Alabama's agricultural history.
It is about Alabama's agricultural development.
It is about Native American farming practices.
It is about the diversification of Alabama's economy.
It is about the role of the Alabama Extension Service.
It is about the role of the Alabama Polytechnic Institute.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the Alabama Extension Service.
It was influenced by the Works Progress Administration (WPA).
It was influenced by the Alabama State Fair.
It was influenced by P. O. Davis.
It was influenced by Vice President Warren Leech.
It was influenced by H.T. Baldwin.
It was influenced by Donald Robertson.
It was influenced by the need for educational communication.
It was influenced by the economic crisis of the Great Depression.
Influenced: It influenced farmers' focus on a vision of the future.
It influenced public interest in the arts.
It influenced the perception of Alabama agriculture.
